headline: Birth registration gap leaves 50,000 children undocumented
context: NRD estimates suggested approximately 50,000 children in Malaysia lacked birth registration as of 2025, primarily in Sabah and rural Peninsular areas.

cards.big: 50,000 children in Malaysia have no birth registration or identity documents
cards.sub: Without birth certificates, children cannot access education or healthcare

cards.big: Births and Deaths Registration Act 1957 requires registration within 14 days
cards.sub: Remote communities and undocumented parents face insurmountable barriers

cards.big: CRC Article 7 guarantees every child the right to registration from birth
cards.sub: Malaysia's CRC reservation on citizenship does not exempt birth registration duty

cards.big: 68% of unregistered children are in Sabah and Sarawak's remote areas
cards.sub: NRD mobile units visit rural areas only 2-3 times annually

cards.big: 50,000 invisible children reflect a system designed for urban populations only
UNICEF Malaysia partnered with NRD for mobile registration in 12 districts in 2024
